what_it_does: Records metadata for an image attachment (id att-b04325ae) linked to the design-source channel, pointing to image://design-source/company-info/p-04 and recording an added timestamp.
observations: YAML frontmatter only, no body content
target uses a custom image:// URI scheme
added timestamp is ISO 8601 with timezone
standouts: Custom image:// URI for the target asset
Attachment is typed as images but has no caption or alt text
concerns: Target URI cannot be resolved from this file alone
No description or alt text for the image
notes: This is a ledger attachment entry that ties an image asset to the design-source channel with a timestamp and a custom URI target.
questions: Where is the image asset physically stored?
Is the image:// URI resolvable by the consuming system?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment ledger entry and does exactly that: it records an image attachment reference for a channel.
intention_signals: It is trying to register an image asset as an attachment to the design-source channel so it can be discovered alongside channel content.
novelty: Use of an image:// URI scheme to reference the target asset rather than a relative file path or HTTP URL.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed attachment record that links an image asset identifier to the design-source channel via a URI target.
summary_for_embedding: A channel-memory attachment metadata file that registers an image asset (target image://design-source/company-info/p-04) under the design-source channel with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: To record and expose an image attachment associated with the design-source channel for downstream discovery and fusion.
